task_updates.executor_notes: Created `supabase/migrations/20260424000000_schedule_discord_promoter.sql` using the repo's existing `pg_cron` DO-block pattern. The migration schedules `discord-resource-promoter` every 10 minutes with direct SQL `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources();` and does not use `net.http_post` or Authorization headers. Verification was file-level for this task: I re-opened the new migration and confirmed the scheduled body is direct SQL. Cross-batch verification after the edit: `banodoco-website` still builds and its full Vitest suite passes; repo-wide `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` still reports the same 7 pre-existing failures already documented in prior batches.

Created `banodoco-website/src/components/resources/AssetDescription.tsx` as a thin wrapper around the existing `MarkdownRenderer`, with the requested single `markdown` prop and null-safe empty handling via `markdown ?? ''`. This keeps the exact existing `react-markdown` + `remarkGfm` pipeline and adds no sanitize/rehype layer or embed-token behavior. Verification: `npm run build` succeeds and `npm test -- --run` passes with 17 test files / 100 tests.

Created `banodoco-website/src/hooks/useAssetComments.ts` with a single Supabase query rooted at `asset_comments` that joins the author member row and nested `asset_comment_media -> media` rows. The hook filters `asset_comments.is_deleted = false` and `asset_comment_media.is_deleted = false`, orders comments by `discord_created_at ASC`, orders nested media by `sort_order`, and returns `discord_guild_id`, `discord_thread_id`, `discord_message_id`, `reply_to_comment_id`, `reply_to_discord_message_id`, `reaction_count`, `content`, and `discord_edited_at` on each normalized comment row. Verification: `npm run build` succeeds after the hook was added, `npm test -- --run` passes with 17 test files / 100 tests, and repo-wide `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` still shows only the same 7 pre-existing failures from earlier batches.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `20260424000000_schedule_discord_promoter.sql` schedules `discord-resource-promoter` every 10 minutes via `cron.schedule(..., $$ SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources(); $$)` inside a `pg_cron` DO block, with no `net.http_post` and no Authorization header.

Yes. `AssetDescription` was created at `banodoco-website/src/components/resources/AssetDescription.tsx` and reuses the existing `MarkdownRenderer`, so it keeps the established `react-markdown` + `remarkGfm` pipeline only, with no sanitize/rehype plugins and no custom embed-token implementation.

Yes. `useAssetComments` now issues a single `asset_comments` query with nested author and `asset_comment_media -> media` joins, filters `is_deleted=false` on both the comment and junction layers, orders by `discord_created_at ASC`, and includes `discord_guild_id`, `discord_thread_id`, `discord_message_id`, `reply_to_comment_id`, and `reply_to_discord_message_id` on each normalized comment row.
